agent1_raw: The benchmark has four flaws. First, the incumbent was calibrated for 90-day renewal probability while the challenger is evaluated on net contribution margin — different objectives, so the comparison doesn't establish which is better on a shared metric. Second, the DR nuisance models were fit on all four quarters including Q3-Q4, which is the reporting window — the evaluator is not independent of the data it's evaluating. Third, the operating cutoff was selected to maximize margin on Q3-Q4 and then reported on Q3-Q4 — circular threshold selection. Fourth, the challenger had three rounds of Bayesian tuning while the incumbent has been frozen for 18 months — asymmetric optimization.
agent2_raw: Four issues make this benchmark insufficient. Objective mismatch: renewal probability versus net contribution margin. DR estimator trained on the reporting window: not a clean holdout evaluation. Threshold tuned on the reporting cohort: inflates apparent performance. Asymmetric calibration effort: fresh Bayesian tuning for challenger versus stale 18-month calibration for incumbent. The 4.5% program value estimate cannot be trusted until these are corrected in a clean protocol.
agent3_raw: The readout conflates four distinct benchmark problems: different objectives across policies, holdout contamination in the DR estimator, threshold selection on the evaluation slice, and asymmetric optimization. Regional propensity variation (0.15-0.62) with randomized assignment within region-week cells is not the problem — doubly robust estimation handles that. The problem is that the estimator was trained on the period it evaluates, and the cutoff was selected on that same period. A clean protocol would require refit nuisance models on pre-reporting data, a predeclared threshold, and an updated incumbent calibration.
synthesis_raw: All three agents agree on empirical_test_agreed. The four issues are consistently identified: objective mismatch, DR evaluator trained on reporting window, threshold tuned on reporting slice, asymmetric optimization. None incorrectly challenges the regional propensity variation as invalidating doubly robust evaluation. ETD: refit nuisance models excluding reporting period, re-calibrate incumbent to shared margin objective, preselect threshold, evaluate on frozen holdout.
all_issues_raised: Objective changed from renewal probability to net contribution margin
DR nuisance models trained on full experiment log including Q3-Q4 reporting period
Operating cutoff selected on Q3-Q4 cohort used for headline reporting
Asymmetric optimization: Bayesian tuning for challenger, frozen 18-month calibration for incumbent

empirical_test.condition: Refit nuisance models on pre-reporting data, preselect threshold, re-calibrate incumbent to margin objective, evaluate both on frozen holdout with block-aware uncertainty by region and week
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger lift falls below zero or CI includes zero on clean holdout
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats re-optimized incumbent by at least 3% incremental profit with block-aware 95% CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive but sub-3% lift, or heterogeneous gains by region with some negative
